title: Those Evening Bells. (Air.--The Bells Of St. Petersburgh.)
content:
Those evening bells! those evening bells!
    How many a tale their music tells,
    Of youth and home and that sweet time
    When last I heard their soothing chime.

    Those joyous hours are past away:
    And many a heart, that then was gay.
    Within the tomb now darkly dwells,
    And hears no more those evening bells.

    And so 'twill be when I am gone:
    That tuneful peal will still ring on,
    While other bards shall walk these dells,
    And sing your praise, sweet evening bells!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Moore delivers a powerful performance in "Those Evening Bells. (Air.--The Bells Of St. Petersburgh.)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
